stories.headline: Russian missile and drone strikes kill two in Kyiv after Zelensky warns of massive attack
stories.summary: Russian missile and drone strikes rocked Kyiv early Thursday, killing two people and wounding more than a dozen, after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a 'massive attack'.

stories.headline: Russia unleashes huge missile and drone attacks on Kyiv
stories.summary: Bombardment follows Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s warning over Moscow preparing ‘massive strike’

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone strikes in Ukraine capital kill at least eight and injure dozens
stories.summary: Multiple explosions heard in Kyiv with a hotel and several residential buildings on fireRussia launched a large-scale attack on Ukraine’s capital Kyiv with missiles and drones overnight into Thursday, killing at least eight people and injuring 34 others.The intense strikes hit residential ⁠buildings and ⁠triggered ​a fire in a hotel on a central boulevard. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Kyiv attacked after Ukraine’s Zelenskyy warns of ‘massive Russian strike’
stories.summary: At least two dead and 11 injured as Ukraine's forces defend against ballistic missiles, drones targeting capital city.

stories.headline: At least eight killed in major missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
stories.summary: Russia launched its biggest strikes on the city in weeks

stories.headline: Watch: Couple scale Empire State Building, get engaged and are arrested
stories.summary: The two were atop the 1,454 ft (443 m) building for at least ten minutes and displayed a large banner before climbing down.

stories.headline: ‘A war zone’: Venezuela aid workers fear health crisis after earthquakes
stories.summary: Aid workers fear disease outbreaks in temporary shelters amid overcrowding, lack of access to clean water, sanitation.

stories.headline: Business Jet Departures in Middle East Drop 30% During Iran War
stories.summary: Business jet departures in the Middle East declined about 30% in the aftermath of the Iran war, a trend that’s at risk of continuing until a peace deal is signed.

stories.headline: China’s ethnic unity law denounced as ‘forced assimilation’ by rights groups
stories.summary: Law comes into effect that critics fear will further erode rights of Uyghurs and Tibetans, as well as allow Beijing to pursue dissidents abroadA new ethnic unity law has come into effect in China despite warnings from Taiwan, the United Nations and rights groups that it could threaten freedoms, especially for minorities.The Law on Promoting Ethnic Unity and Progress aims to forge a “shared” national identity among ethnic groups, for example by st

stories.headline: Venezuela's death toll at least 2,295 as medical crisis widens
stories.summary: Doctors fear a severe medical crisis in Venezuela following the 24 June earthquakes that killed 2,295 and injured more than 11,000. Thousands of displaced survivors are living in crowded shelters without clean water.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: Warsh Must Beware of Curves Flattening to Deceive
stories.summary: The bond market is taking him seriously, but that’s not necessarily great news.

stories.headline: US Tech Rout Sours Mood For Japan, South Korean Chip Stocks
stories.summary: Japanese and South Korean semiconductor stocks tumbled Thursday after a selloff in US technology shares soured the mood across Asia. Concerns over greater competition increased after Apple Inc. was said to be in negotiations to buy Chinese chips.

stories.headline: Global Appetite for Korea Grows Ahead of 24-Hour Won Trading
stories.summary: Global investors’ interest in South Korea’s economy and capital markets has “clearly changed” for the better in recent years, the country’s top finance ministry official said, as the won prepares to shift to round-the-clock trading on July 6.

stories.headline: Iran war live: ‘Positive progress’ as US, Tehran wrap up indirect talks
stories.summary: Tehran says a 'communication channel' will be established with Washington to report and discuss breaches of the MoU.

stories.headline: South Korea embraces 24-hour won trading in break with currency 'trauma'
South Korean exports in June soar past $100bn for first time on chip demand
Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed a AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.headline: Trump’s billion-dollar makeover of Washington DC
stories.summary: The US president calls it a ‘gift’ to the nation. Critics say he is racing past checks to stamp his mark on the capital

stories.headline: Headline's Zhu on Asia Tech Opportunities
stories.summary: Clarey Zhu, Partner at Headline Growth, discusses the investment opportunities in Asia's tech sector, including AI infrastructure, deep tech and robotics. She speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts from the sidelines of the IVS2026 conference in Kyoto. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Citi's Luet: China's Franchise Has Grown Very Strongly
stories.summary: Marc Luet, Head of Japan, Asia North & Australia and Banking at Citi, says the bank has seen the role of the yuan grow and is hopeful of obtaining a securities license in China. He speaks exclusively on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran’s Floating Oil Stockpile Swells as Major Buyers Stay Away
stories.summary: A hoard of Iranian oil is building up at sea, as the Islamic Republic struggles to find buyers before the expiry of a 60-day window granted by Washington.

stories.headline: Korean Stocks Fall as Jitters Over Meta and Apple Hit Chipmakers
stories.summary: South Korean stocks slumped as Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell computing power raised questions over excess in AI capacity, driving a selloff in chipmakers.

stories.headline: Iron Ore Advances as CMRG May Block Some Fortescue Deliveries
stories.summary: Iron ore advanced — with futures briefly topping $100 a ton — after China’s state-backed buyer of the commodity signaled that it planned to restrict some Fortescue Ltd. inventories held at mainland ports.

stories.headline: India IPO Pipeline Hits Record as Big Guns Line Up
stories.summary: Bankers expect IPO activity to accelerate in the coming months.

stories.headline: Data Center Firm Switch Seeks $2 Billion in Funding Round
stories.summary: Switch Inc. is kicking off a private funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz that could raise about $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Takaichi to Boost India Ties as China Tensions Rise
stories.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi arrived in India for a three-day trip to deepen economic ties and strengthen security cooperation with counterpart Narendra Modi. Bloomberg's Yoshiaki Nohara breaks down the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Emerging Asia Bonds Draw Global Funds Despite Fed Hike Fears
stories.summary: Foreign investors are piling back into Asian emerging-market bonds despite renewed Federal Reserve hawkishness, as expectations that regional central banks will keep interest rates elevated support the debt’s yield appeal.

stories.headline: Nine Arrested in Federal Crackdown on L.A.’s Sex-Trafficking Corridor
stories.summary: Federal officials in Los Angeles described gang members’ yearslong scheme that relied on social media and physical violence to recruit and maintain control of the girls.

Israel kills three Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: An Israeli drone strike near a Gaza hospital and fuel station kills at least three people, reports Wafa news agency.

stories.summary: Report accuses paramilitary force of crimes including ethnic cleansing in systemic campaign against civiliansThe Sudanese paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during its campaign to capture El Fasher, Amnesty International has alleged.Many of the crimes, including murder, torture, rape, enslavement and sexual slavery, were carried out as part of a widespread and systematic attack against civilia

stories.headline: Chip Selloff Extends to South Korea, Oil Declines: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A selloff in semiconductor stocks spread to South Korea, reviving concerns that the blistering rally in artificial intelligence shares this year may have gone too far, too fast. Brent fell to the lowest since before the start of the Middle East war.

stories.headline: Ukraine launches 'transparent mechanism' allowing partner countries to buy weapons, Kyiv says
stories.summary: According to the most recent reports, Ukraine is now producing over 4 million drones annually and could double that number with sufficient funding.

stories.headline: Ukraine’s former commander-in-chief to run for president, reports say
stories.summary: Valerii Zaluzhnyi, former commander-in-chief of Ukraine's Armed Forces and now Kyiv’s envoy to the UK, is reportedly willing to run in the next presidential election once the situation allows. Zaluzhnyi is seen as Zelenskyy’s main rival.

stories.headline: Couple arrested after daring Empire State marriage proposal stunt
stories.summary: Russian daredevils Vanya Beerkus and Angela Nikolau are seen as ambassadors for 'rooftopping', a climbing sport.

stories.headline: Russian couple arrested after climbing Empire State Building
stories.summary: Two Russian climbers scaled New York’s Empire State Building on Wednesday as part of a death-defying marriage proposal.

stories.headline: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
stories.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

stories.headline: Controversial bishops ordained as Pope warns of 'schism' in Catholic Church
stories.summary: Thousands of worshippers attended a ceremony in the Swiss Alps as part of the breakaway Society of Saint Pius X.

stories.headline: 'Time for him to pay' - Carroll calls on Trump to pay $5m after president's appeal fails
stories.summary: The writer's lawyers say Trump wants to delay paying the damages awarded by a jury that found the president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her.

stories.headline: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?
stories.summary: Rights groups fear the new law gives Beijing legal authority to go after people beyond its borders.

stories.headline: US opts not to renew Trump’s trade deal with Mexico and Canada
stories.summary: Washington will instead hold annual reviews on terms of commerce with its biggest trading partners

stories.headline: Traders Plot Worst-Case Scenario for Yen If Crisis Hits
stories.summary: The yen sliding to a once-unthinkable 200 per dollar level is now a medium-term risk — albeit an extreme one — for some investors.

stories.headline: Judge Blocks Postal Service From Imposing Restrictions on Mail-In Ballots
stories.summary: The ruling, based on agreements the service made in a 2021 legal settlement, was the second time a judge has blocked new curbs by President Trump on voting by mail.

stories.headline: Crypto Brought Trump a Huge Windfall, Even as Many Investors Lost Big
stories.summary: President Trump and his family reaped vast financial rewards from a memecoin that generated losses for hundreds of thousands of investors.

stories.headline: Venezuelan police officers arrested over alleged looting after earthquakes

In a moment captured by fishers off Venezuela’s Caribbean coast, two earthquakes struck seconds apart. stories.headline: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
stories.summary: President Trump has sought to limit mail-in voting and has ordered his administration to impose limits on the practice.

stories.headline: State of emergency: Bolivia’s currency plummets as anger simmers
stories.summary: Bolivia’s President Paz declared a state of emergency after 50 days of protests against his policies.

stories.headline: Albania NATO summit hangs in balance as US slams security 'free riders'
stories.summary: The proposed host country of next year's meeting hasn't shown credible progress on the alliance's new defence spending target of 5 percent of GDP.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: Greek politician's mother dies of wounds after arson attack
stories.summary: Three separate attacks involving petrol bombs and other improvised devices took place in Thessaloniki in the early hours of Wednesday.

stories.headline: Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
stories.summary: The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Caraballeda, La Guaira, where locals are still desperately searching for family members in the rubble.

stories.summary: Guidance to be announced as soon as next week after government intervention in Anthropic and OpenAI rollouts

stories.headline: Trump made up to $1.4bn in stock purchases in 2025
stories.summary: FT analysis shows US president declared more than 22,000 share transactions last year

stories.headline: Perpetual Rejects $1.7 Billion Takeover Bid From PE Firm EQT
stories.summary: Perpetual Ltd. has rejected a takeover offer from a company indirectly controlled by Swedish private equity firm EQT AB, the latest in a string of refusals to various suitors by the Australian asset manager.

stories.headline: Voters are angry with Washington, and other takeaways from the Colorado primaries
stories.summary: A sitting Democratic House member and a sitting senator both lost key races in the state.

stories.headline: Israeli opposition signal foreign policy change in style, but not substance
stories.summary: Israeli opposition criticise the means employed by Netanyahu, but cannot risk opposing his ends.

stories.headline: As Venezuela responds to earthquake devastation, volunteers take charge
stories.summary: As frustration with the government disaster response grows, everyday citizens are organising to distribute aid.

stories.headline: Russian gas imports rise despite EU phase-out
stories.summary: Russian liquefied natural gas continues to arrive mainly through Spain, France, Belgium and the Netherlands, while pipeline deliveries remain concentrated in Hungary, Slovakia and Greece.

stories.headline: China is a clear winner from Trump’s war in Middle East, report concludes

stories.headline: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
stories.summary: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

stories.headline: Chinese tycoon sentenced to 30 years in US jail
stories.summary: Guo Wengui branded himself as a China critic, gaining followers who backed his fraudulent schemes.

stories.headline: ‘They will attack me if I stay’: immigrants in South Africa flee for safety amid violence and anti-foreigner protests
stories.summary: More than 2,000 anti-foreigner protesters march through Durban city centre as the arbitrary deadline passes for undocumented migrants to leave the countrySouth Africa was holding its breath on Tuesday as mass anti-immigration protests were held across the country. stories.headline: Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
stories.summary: Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.

stories.headline: Manhunt after bomb injures Ukrainian oligarch in Monaco
stories.summary: The blast was caused by an explosive device which appeared to contain bolts and pellets, the head of Monaco's government said.

stories.headline: Founder of Asian super-app Gojek sentenced to years in jail for corruption
stories.summary: The case against Gojek founder Nadiem Makarim relates to a laptop deal made during his term as Indonesia's education minister.

stories.headline: Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
stories.summary: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

stories.headline: What to know about the looming  deadline on North American free trade
stories.summary: The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
stories.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

stories.headline: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
stories.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

stories.headline: Dogs, drones and sound detectors: How rescuers search for quake survivors
stories.summary: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
stories.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward Pride Match
stories.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.

stories.headline: UPS is losing ground to FedEx
stories.summary: The two logistics giants have similar strategies—and face a common threat
